**NH REPUBLICAN PARTY****WEEKLY NEWSLETTER**** _SPECIAL THANKSGIVING EDITION_ WEDNESDAY, NOVEMBER 22ND** **_A Note From Jeanie_** **Dear Derek,**
**Happy Thanksgiving! Republicans in New Hampshire do have much to be thankful for: we have a very popular Republican Governor, majorities in our House, Senate, and on the Executive Council. ********Down the road in Webster, Hale the Turkey is VERY thankful that Governor Sununu pardoned him today at the Executive Council meeting.********Here at the State Party we are thankful for the leadership in Concord that has brought forward a much needed course correction for New Hampshire’s economy and the hardworking folks who live here. ********Thanks to Republicans:**
* **NH has a responsible budget that lives within our means and doesn’t raise or create any new taxes or fees.**
* **The business climate is improving by reducing burdensome taxes on small business.**
* **The rainy day fund has increased to $100 million strengthening our economy and protecting the state from future economic uncertainty.**

******Thanks to Republicans:**
* **NH is fighting the opioid epidemic by assuring that increased funding is going to prevention, treatment, and recovery programs that work.**
* **There has been real progress on school choice, assuring that a child’s access to education is not defined by his or her zip code.**
* **We are helping cities and towns—by sending $37 million back to them for road and bridge projects, additional funds for drinking water and waste water projects, and funds for critical capital projects.**

******Thanks to Republicans:**
* **Improving treatment of mental health is now a priority.**
* **Protecting those most in need including strengthening the rape shield law, requiring sex offenders to report their online profiles, or expanding the criminal background check for foster families.**
* **Protecting our constitutional rights by strengthening the integrity of our elections.**

******NH Republicans are on the right track and getting great things accomplished.********As you gather with family and friends tomorrow, we wish you a wonderful Thanksgiving! And, if you get a chance, be sure to thank a Republican by tweeting out one or more of the accomplishments listed above using the hashtag [#ThankYouNHGOP](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=3qP3xl6Iy86fMdiFlFopyKcu5HJVYnv1VILoX5OLKe8-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MbbN-2FSNDnTxLYaKYQkdfL9MOA50j4GyUyqJ58fBwia4soOvDea9X-2F1MUIEsez6DAOtn4p-2F1fSqESmuL3e8d4zKsPpGqw-2FrZibHaJwUQIe-2BpybA7UU7VrQ1Gb9zoJZlaLW146zYgVGZsjfl0fkpylg9QGjv83skcGyNN8bbiKQk7dZxxKSggXIJYS-2BihmaXJLr5K3LqxEyHxbHmv-2BL09NXzQB1p593jm1ofIF5hAXTDMhSqeQ2HYJUOIbWPgZLWBJ9yCYP0ub-2FiB1arbdUW-2BsjbQ-3D-3D). **

Friend,

Thanksgiving kicks off the long-awaited holiday season each year with delicious food and plenty of football, but it's also a reminder to pause and think about the blessings in our lives.

I always look forward to sitting down at Thanksgiving dinner and listening as my family recounts what they are thankful for. This year, I am especially thankful for the privilege to be governor of the great State of Delaware, and for the service and sacrifice that our men and women in uniform give each day.

Delaware is a special place, and I am thankful for the chance to work with each of you to help make it even better.

Let's give thanks today and every day. I hope you have a wonderful and blessed Thanksgiving.

John

name: Gov. Mike Huckabee, My Faith Votes
subject: Here’s what I’m thankful for…
body:
| | Happy Thanksgiving from everyone at My Faith Votes
---
[ ](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Fdonate&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171123_tg_s1&n=1)
| Peter, I love the Thanksgiving season, a time when we slow down and ponder the blessings God has given us. Thanksgiving is distinctively American. Not because of the turkey dinner or the football game...but instead because of our Judeo-Christian faith. We remember the first Thanksgiving in America where the grateful puritans joined with their Indian friends for a three-day thanksgiving festival. Yet, what many people don’t know is that the Thanksgiving celebrations didn’t become common in America until the American Revolution when Congress issued 15 prayer and Thanksgiving proclamations. So it is appropriate that with the commencement of the federal government in 1789, Congress first wanted to return thanks to Almighty God and delivered a Congressional Thanksgiving Proclamation to George Washington’s desk which he signed making Thursday, the 26th day of November the Thanksgiving holiday we enjoy today. Thanksgiving was created by people of faith, puritans, and patriots, to return thanks to God. Reflecting on this past year, we have so much to be thankful for. We saw Christians across America come together and pray – and God answered our prayers. All across America the values of life, marriage, family, and religious liberty experienced victories because of the leaders you voted into office from the federal level all the way down to the local level. As we remember the history of Thanksgiving let us also note our role in history. Our chapter looks different from the Puritans or those who fought in the American Revolution. They laid the groundwork for us in founding a nation upon Judeo-Christian principles. Our job is to be responsible stewards of what they faithfully built and God generously blessed. I’m thankful for you, your family and the thousands of other Christians who make up My Faith Votes. Together, in 2016, you helped shape history with your vote as a bold declaration for God. And I believe My Faith Votes is on the cusp of writing history in 2018 when 90 million Christians stand together to vote. I’m so grateful to be working with you this coming year to make God and his principles known in our nation. Since I was a pastor before I was ever a politician, I want to leave you with a verse from Colossians. “And whatever you do, in word or deed, do everything in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father through him.” (Colossians 3:17) Thanksgiving is a time where we reflect on God’s goodness to us this past year. The answered prayers, the lessons God has taught us, and remember the blessings God has given us as we spend time with family and friends. All while remaining prayerful of what God can and will do in the coming year. I pray God will continue to use us - millions of Christians across the United States. And I pray God blesses you and your family this Thanksgiving as we thank God for our faith and our freedom. **United. We Stand.**

Gov. Mike Huckabee
Honorary National Chairman
[](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171123_tg_s1&n=2)
[Support My Faith Votes with your donation today](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Fdonate&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171123_tg_s1&n=3) _My Faith Votes is a non-partisan movement focused on motivating Christians in America to participate in local and national elections and be active members of society. By partnering with local churches, pastors, and national faith leaders, My Faith Votes mobilizes and equips Christians to lead the conversation on the place of faith in culture and politics._

name: Robert Becker via Our Revolution
subject: UPDATE: Aid to Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands
body:
#
Friends,
Having just returned to my home in Iowa after spending 40 days in Vieques, Puerto Rico, I am thankful for electricity, running water you can drink and internet connectivity.
Now a full two months after Hurricane Maria obliterated Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands, most of the Commonwealth's 3.5 million American citizens will celebrate this Thanksgiving without power or water.
What began as a climate change fueled natural disaster has become a man-made humanitarian crisis because of the Trump administration's callous disregard for the lives of our citizens.
There is no doubt in my mind that if Puerto Rico were home to multinational fossil fuel headquarters or the wealthy estates of the vulture capitalists behind their crippling debt crisis, the lights would be on by now. But in Donald Trump's world, the lives of Puerto Ricans and Virgin Islanders don't count.
Fortunately, despite Trump's indifference, heroes are emerging through the thankless work of private citizens working around the clock to save lives. I saw these heroes firsthand -- doctors and nurses treating medical patients in outdoor tents because the hospital on Vieques had been destroyed... volunteers going house to house to make sure elderly residents and families had food and water... US veterans toiling to get generators working to power the island's water supply and sewage treatment.
And while the Washington politicians ignore this crisis, local political leaders are stepping up... Vieques Vice Mayor Daisy Cruz has been tirelessly overseeing the municipality's emergency response with a mission to get the island's schools reopened and San Juan Mayor Carmen Yulin Cruz has become a forceful voice for justice.
And our very own Sen. Bernie Sanders came to San Juan -- not to brag and throw toilet paper at Puerto Ricans as Trump did -- but to simply listen and learn.
[**The heroes of Puerto Rico 's and the U.S. Virgin Islands' recovery still need our help. Please consider making a donation this Thanksgiving to help relief efforts.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ae11/bed43eb/2c15be5b/75ff593e/4067472127/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/puerto-rico?refcode=em171123-OurRev")
By working together we can do right by our fellow citizens.
In solidarity,
Robert Becker
Former Iowa Director, Bernie 2016
Our Revolution member

| Dear Peter,
Happy, Happy Thanksgiving to you and your family. I hope you have a special celebration with the many blessings of the holiday.
We all remember the story of the 1621 Thanksgiving celebration in Plymouth, Massachusetts, from which we draw much of our modern tradition.
But did you know that two years earlier, [settlers celebrated Thanksgiving here in Virginia](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001HImwVmwxpAcjTg9SFvj_Yr7yVFQ7z0VY_fAGyf-PWSsnrDEUQp9MPgNcrNJbsrVxMkaknKMJDn8dXz_QN8EGd1xEmZ8nOV_hVCLTeRJh1EQw_oPEgXrASP8lBNb7p5Fe1rfKV-tBfc9N08i_G2TcjQ7wVpVf3E5R1Kn4xwKQMVK40uN56yIY669Z0Yd605I6&c=kfrttGFdJnI8_R52A6SYajmUvxQLyeQzeRrx3qNDIZ_lnQHQE9cjFw==&ch=V_mXZw_m0sk1qKyKM1kB9gS8DckNd66NTR3HvrLQrL5SUuIxp82Wkg==), at Berkeley Plantation, in what is now Charles City County? After a long and dangerous journey across the ocean, and without turkey or pumpkin pie, they fell to their knees in thankful prayer, declaring:
"We ordaine that this day of our ships arrival, at the place assigned for plantacon, in the land of Virginia, shall be yearly and perpetually kept holy as a day of Thanksgiving to Almighty God." - Captain John Woodlief, Dec. 4th, 1619
Thanksgiving was not invented in Virginia, though. Other settlers celebrated even earlier Thanksgivings in Maine in 1607, in Texas in 1598, and in Florida in 1565.
Lacking newspapers to learn of the celebrations of others, these settlers, hailing from different nations, speaking different languages, sharing different religious traditions, and arriving in different decades -- and after much hardship along the way -- nevertheless reached the same conclusion: they were in a land of limitless opportunity, and for that, should remain thankful in eternity.
In 1789, in the first year of his Presidency, George Washington called for this nation to give thanks, writing on what would become one of our most cherished American traditions:
"...we may then all unite in rendering unto him our sincere and humble thanks--for his kind care and protection of the People of this Country previous to their becoming a Nation--for the signal and manifold mercies, and the favorable interpositions of his Providence which we experienced in the course and conclusion of the late war--for the great degree of tranquility, union, and plenty, which we have since enjoyed--for the peaceable and rational manner, in which we have been enabled to establish constitutions of government for our safety and happiness, and particularly the national One now lately instituted--for the civil and religious liberty with which we are blessed..." - George Washington
George Washington's call to give thanks is one tradition we wisely kept.
On this day of heartfelt celebration, may you and your family receive the best of God's blessings and find new reasons to be thankful in the year ahead. Happy, Happy Thanksgiving!

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
body:
#
Hello,

There are so many things I'm grateful for in my life. In May, my wife and I welcomed our third child, Davis, and our twins Caroline and Jackson continue to make us proud as they grow and learn their place in the world. I'm truly blessed with a house of love.

I'm grateful for the grace of the people of Tallahassee, and people all across Florida. On this journey, we've had a chance to meet thousands of people who have shared their stories of triumph, their big dreams, and their hopes for their children. They've given us strength and hope that our state's best days are still ahead of us.

And I'm grateful for the richness of our experience, especially during trying times. Our collective strength far outweighs the difficulties we might face, and I've never been more convinced of that than I am today.

Happy Thanksgiving from my family to yours,

Andrew

name: Rob Portman
subject: Happy Thanksgiving
body:
| Peter \--

Happy Thanksgiving! Jane and I are thankful for your friendship, and we hope you and your family have a wonderful holiday weekend.

Whether you are enjoying a delicious meal, watching football, or just enjoying each other's company, we hope you can take some time away to reflect and give thanks for the many blessings in our lives.

Please join us in taking a moment to say a prayer of thanks for those who protect this country at home and abroad, many of whom are unable to be home with their families this Thanksgiving.

Happy Thanksgiving, and we hope to see you soon.

All best,

Rob Portman [](http://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.facebook.com%2Frobportman&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=thanksgiving_17&n=1)[](http://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2Frobportman&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=thanksgiving_17&n=2)[

name: Maggie Hassan
subject: Happy Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hello,
Tom, Meg, Ben, and I have a lot to be thankful for this Thanksgiving. We're fortunate to have our family, our friends, our state, our country, and much more.
As each of us prepares to sit down for turkey, gravy, and pumpkin pie with our neighbors and loved ones, it's more important than ever that we lend a hand where we're able and lift one another up. One way to do that is by supporting the New Hampshire Food Bank.
If you have a little extra to spare, please donate what you can so that others can enjoy a warm meal: [**https://www.nhfoodbank.org/donation-hub/**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdfa6/2a380d31/32bbe53/fe2820/3946698366/VEsH/ "https://www.nhfoodbank.org/donation-hub/")
Wishing you and yours a very happy Thanksgiving.
With every good wish,
Maggie Hassan

name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: Happy Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail
body:
| |
---
| |
---
| | [](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e75/622b7b5b/7bd7682/307ba7c7/3872968879/VEsH/ "https://www.instagram.com/cleancookingcongresswoman/")
---
P.S. Cooking is a major part of my life, and cooking on Thanksgiving is more than just a standard tradition. As your "Clean Cooking Congresswoman," I wanted to share a recipe I developed with fruits and one of my favorite cooking methods - the slow cooker.

**Kumquat and Cherry Chicken with Roasted Vegetables
Recipe by [@cleancookingcongresswoman](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e75/622b7b5b/7bd7682/307ba7c7/3872968879/VEsE/ "https://www.instagram.com/cleancookingcongresswoman/")**

2 chicken (or turkey) breasts

1 green bell pepper

1 red bell pepper

10 kumquats

10 Orange Glory grape tomatoes (yellow cherry or grape tomatoes are also fine)

10 Rainier cherries

1 tbsp honey

1-2 tbsp Mina Harissa spicy sauce (depending on taste)

1/2 c water

Salt and pepper, to taste

Directions:

Cut up raw chicken/turkey breast into small pieces, slice bell peppers, cut kumquats and grape tomatoes in half, and slice cherries and remove pits. Place chicken, fruit and vegetables in slow cooker. Add honey, Mina Harissa spicy sauce, water, and salt and pepper. Mix thoroughly. Turn slow cooker on low for 4-6 hours. Stir every hour and check after 3 hours for poultry doneness.

name: Jay Inslee
subject: Thank you.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
From my family to yours, thank you.

| [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MTI0MTEwNjAS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MTk5NDE3MwS2&mt=1&rt=0) Dear Friend, In reflecting on this past year, it's easy to get lost in the valleys and forget to recognize the peaks. So, I'd like to say: Thank you. Thank you for your determination and persistence through headline after headline of news nobody wanted to hear. Thank you for resisting the president's agenda at every turn. Without you, we couldn't have saved Obamacare or rallied against the Muslim Ban. Thank you for having my back as we've taken aggressive action to combat climate change and made Washington an example of a clean energy economy for the world. Thank you for electing progressive leadership in Washington state to complete the Great Blue Wall. Before our families gather around the table for dinner, I wanted to make sure you know: **We are grateful for all you 've done.** [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTI0MTEwNjAS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MTk5NDE3MwS2&mt=1&rt=0) We appreciate you so much, and we hope that you have a wonderful and restful weekend with friends and family. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee [**P.S.
